title: Today's AI Brief: Anthropic's Lease and OpenAI's Gartner Win
summary: Anthropic's lease with SpaceX sparks debate, OpenAI named a Leader in enterprise coding agents by Gartner
imageAlt: Image from source article for How long is Anthropic’s lease with SpaceX? Opinions vary.
imageCaption: How long is Anthropic’s lease with SpaceX? Opinions vary.
imageSourceTitle: How long is Anthropic’s lease with SpaceX? Opinions vary.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI Brief. Let's dive into some fresh news. First up, there's a lot of chatter about Anthropic's lease with SpaceX. Apparently, there's some debate about how long this lease actually is. I am looking at a report from TechCrunch that says xAI signed a major compute deal with Anthropic earlier this month, pledging billions of dollars a month for exclusive use of the company’s Colossus cluster. My read is that this deal is a big win for both companies, giving xAI some much-needed computing power.

The interesting part here is that opinions vary on the length of the lease. I'm not sure what's going on behind the scenes, but it's clear that both Anthropic and xAI are making some big bets on AI. What stands out to me is that this deal shows just how much computing power is needed to train and run these massive AI models.

Also on the board, OpenAI just got named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Enterprise AI Coding Agents. Their Codex platform was recognized for innovation and enterprise-scale deployment. I think this is a big deal because it shows that OpenAI is making some real progress in the enterprise space.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
